name: Acapulco Chicken Enchilada
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup scallions, white and green parts removed, minced
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, mix together the cooked shredded chicken, scallions, almonds, and salt.
2. To prepare the enchilada sauce, heat vegetable oil in a sauté pan over medium-high heat. Add onion, green bell pepper, and garlic. Sauté until vegetables are soft.
3. Stir in tomato paste, water, chili powder, salt, and dried oregano. Blend well. Reduce heat to medium, cover, and simmer for 5 minutes.
4. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C). Lightly oil the bottom of a shallow ovenproof casserole dish.
5. Dip one tortilla in the hot sauce until it is partially saturated. Place the tortilla in the casserole dish and fill with about 1/8 cup of the chicken mixture, spreading evenly over the tortilla.
6. Add about 1 tablespoon sour cream and roll the enchilada seam side down. Repeat with remaining tortillas.
7. When all the casserole is filled, drizzle any remaining sauce over top. Sprinkle cheese and olives on the dish.
8. Bake in the preheated oven for 15 minutes or until the cheese and sauce are hot and bubbling. Serve with additional sour cream and chopped scallions on the side.
